title: Diff-Review (code-review / pull-request) document
description: Data contract for the diff-review format: an IDE/PR-style code-review document with a changed-file tree, diff hunks (unified or split), inline review threads, reviewers, CI checks, and a live verdict. render() builds the whole three-pane app shell from a valid instance of this schema; nothing is hard-coded in the HTML body.

meta.description: Shared report metadata (brand-neutral chrome). Keep {{ORG}} / {{PROJECT}} / {{AUTHOR}} placeholders unless real values are supplied.

reportType.description: Document class label, e.g. 'Code Review' or 'Pull Request'.

title.description: Document title shown in the app bar.

author.description: Report author / PR opener. Placeholder {{AUTHOR}} by default.

theme.description: Initial brand accent; applied to <html data-theme>. The in-report switcher can change it live.

change.description: The pull-request / change-set being reviewed: title, branches, overall verdict, and a summary body.

title.description: Change / PR title shown in the top bar.

sourceBranch.description: Source branch chip (the head being merged).

targetBranch.description: Target branch chip (the base being merged into).

verdict.description: Overall review verdict driving the top-bar badge; updated live by the approve / request-changes control.

summary.description: Optional review description / rationale (plain text, shown in the right pane).

reviewers.description: Reviewers and their current review state, listed in the right-pane review summary.

state.description: Reviewer's state: approved / changes-requested / pending / commented.

checks.description: CI / status checks listed in the right pane (pass / fail / running).

detail.description: Optional one-line detail, e.g. '248 passed · 0 failed · 31s'.

files.description: Changed files. Each renders one tree row in the left pane and (when selected) the diff in the center pane.

status.description: File change status driving its status dot and the file-status filter chips.

additions.description: Added line count for this file.

deletions.description: Removed line count for this file.

hunks.description: Diff hunks for this file. Each has an @@ header and an ordered list of lines.

header.description: Hunk context label after the @@ markers, e.g. 'class LedgerWriter'.

oldStart.description: First line number in the OLD file for this hunk.

newStart.description: First line number in the NEW file for this hunk.

collapsed.description: Optional: render this hunk collapsed initially.

lines.description: Ordered diff lines. Line numbers are derived/explicit per gutter.

oldNo.description: Line number in the OLD gutter (null/absent for added lines).

newNo.description: Line number in the NEW gutter (null/absent for removed lines).

text.description: Raw line text (without the leading +/-/space marker).

tokens.description: Optional light syntax tinting: ordered span runs that concatenate to `text`. No heavy parser — bake these in the data.

c.description: Optional class: 'kw' keyword, 'str' string, 'com' comment, 'num' number, 'fn' function/type.

threads.description: Inline review threads anchored under a specific diff line in this file.

side.description: Which gutter the anchor line belongs to: 'new' (added/context new-no) or 'old' (removed old-no).

line.description: Line number (in the chosen side's gutter) the thread is anchored to.

time.description: Human time label, e.g. '2h ago' or '14:08'.

resolved.description: Whether the thread is resolved (mutes styling + drops the file comment count).

change.title: fix(ledger): bound retries + DLQ poisoned events
change.summary: Caps ledger-writer retries at 5 with exponential backoff and routes poison-pill events to a dead-letter queue, so a single malformed event can no longer hot-loop and OOMKill the pod (INC-2041).

lines.text: public boolean shouldRetry(Event e, int attempt) {

threads.author: @h.cho
threads.body: MAX_ATTEMPTS is hard-coded to 5 — pull it from config so we can tune per-environment without a redeploy.
